title: paspalum apiculatum
intro: Paspalum apiculatum is a species of grass in the family Poaceae, commonly known as a type of panic grass. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Paspalum apiculatum include treating wounds, reducing fever, and soothing digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are typically dried and then used to make teas, infusions, or poultices, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
